results.title: Agents-K1: Towards Agent-native Knowledge Orchestration
results.content: Unified scientific knowledge infrastructure. We introduce Agents-K1, an agent-native knowledge orchestration pipeline that unifies KG, LLM, and CLI in a unified framework. Unlike static scholarly knowledge graphs, Agents-K1 is designed around the full workflow of research agents: parsing papers, extracting structured knowledge, building reusable graphs, and exposing the resulting knowledge through agent-facing interfaces. [...] Agents-K1 is designed as a reusable knowledge infrastructure for this setting. Instead of asking the agent to reason directly over scattered PDFs and flat search results, Agents-K1 first converts documents into structured multimodal knowledge graphs, then exposes these graphs through retrieval and tool interfaces that an agent can use during research. In this way, the framework separates two jobs that are often mixed together: offline construction of reliable knowledge representations, and online use of this knowledge for evidence-grounded reasoning. [...] To this end, we present Agents-K1, an end-to-end knowledge orchestration pipeline that converts raw documents into agent-native multimodal knowledge graphs at scale. Agents-K1 integrates a multimodal parser, a five-module extraction schema, a reinforcement-learned extraction model, and a tri-source agent CLI into a single framework, and the same pipeline can be replayed on arbitrary corpora. The first stage is multimodal parsing and schema (Section 4). It uses a MinerU-based offline parser to ingest PDFs and a five-module schema (metadata, explicit mentions, implicit abstractions, citation intent, and fine-grained inter-entity relations) that organises the entire paper rather than its abstract alone, with figures, tables, and equations admitted as first-class evidence alongside textual

results.title: [論文評述] Agents-K1: Towards Agent-native Knowledge Orchestration
results.content:
The paper "Agents-K1: Towards Agent-native Knowledge Orchestration" introduces an end-to-end pipeline designed to convert raw scientific documents into agent-native scientific knowledge graphs (KGs), addressing the limitations of existing LLM-based research agents in scientific knowledge orchestration. Current systems often reduce papers to abstracts, superficial mentions, and flat citation edges, failing to capture critical scientific entities, claims, evidence, mechanisms, and method lineages necessary for robust scientific reasoning. Agents-K1 aims to provide structured, auditable knowledge across the full paper, integrating text, figures, tables, and equations as connected evidence. [...] The Agents-K1 framework comprises three coordinated components: a Knowledge Graph (KG) Layer, an LLM Layer, and a Command-Line Interface (CLI) Layer.

1. KG Layer: End-to-end Pipeline for Scientific Knowledge Construction (Section 4)
This layer parses full papers and extracts multimodal content, organizing it into structured knowledge graphs. [...] `Region`
`Geoscience Variables`

In conclusion, Agents-K1 provides a unified, agent-native knowledge orchestration pipeline for scientific literature, delivering million-scale multimodal knowledge graphs, a reinforcement-learned extraction backbone, and an executable graph-based research toolkit (`GraphAnything CLI`), significantly enhancing multi-hop scientific reasoning and auditable knowledge tracing.

`GraphAnything CLI`

results.title: Agents-K1: Towards Agent-native Knowledge Orchestration
results.content:
Current LLM-based research agents have advanced through agent orchestration, yet largely overlook scientific knowledge orchestration. Existing works often reduce papers to abstracts, surface mentions, and flat \texttt{cites} edges, omitting key entities, claims, evidence, mechanisms, and method lineages essential for scientific reasoning. To this end, we introduce Agents-K1, an end-to-end knowledge orchestration pipeline that converts raw documents into agent-native scientific knowledge graphs. Agents-K1 integrates three components under a unifying theoretical foundation: a multimodal parser whose five-module schema captures entities, multimodal evidence, citations, and typed inter-entity relations across the full paper rather than abstracts alone; a 4B information-extraction backbone [...] than abstracts alone; a 4B information-extraction backbone trained with GRPO under a rule-based reward; and a graphanything CLI, a tri-source agent interface that unifies web search, multimodal graph retrieval, and cross-document traversal. On top of this, we process 2.46 million scientific papers across six subjects to produce Scholar-KG, of which we release a one-million-paper subset, and the full Scholar-KG is accessible via the SCP link below. The same pipeline can be extended to general-domain corpora and to schema-conformant data synthesis. Extensive experiments demonstrate that Agents-K1 achieves superior performance in scientific information extraction, knowledge graph construction, and multi-hop scientific reasoning. [...] Sign in

results.title: Agents-K1: Towards Agent-native Knowledge Orchestration | Cool Papers - Immersive Paper Discovery
results.content:
Current LLM-based research agents have advanced through agent orchestration, yet largely overlook scientific knowledge orchestration. Existing works often reduce papers to abstracts, surface mentions, and flat \texttt{cites} edges, omitting key entities, claims, evidence, mechanisms, and method lineages essential for scientific reasoning. To this end, we introduce \textbf{Agents-K1}, an end-to-end knowledge orchestration pipeline that converts raw documents into agent-native scientific knowledge graphs. Agents-K1 integrates three components under a unifying theoretical foundation: a multimodal parser whose five-module schema captures entities, multimodal evidence, citations, and typed inter-entity relations across the full paper rather than abstracts alone; a 4B information-extraction [...] rather than abstracts alone; a 4B information-extraction backbone trained with GRPO under a rule-based reward; and a graphanything CLI, a tri-source agent interface that unifies web search, multimodal graph retrieval, and cross-document traversal. On top of this, we process 2.46 million scientific papers across six subjects to produce \textbf{Scholar-KG}, of which we release a one-million-paper subset, and the full Scholar-KG is accessible via the SCP link below. The same pipeline can be extended to general-domain corpora and to schema-conformant data synthesis. Extensive experiments demonstrate that Agents-K1 achieves superior performance in scientific information extraction, knowledge graph construction, and multi-hop scientific reasoning. results.title: Agents-K1: Towards Agent-native Knowledge Orchestration
results.content:
## Conclusion

Agents-K1 represents a shift toward building infrastructure that treats AI agents as primary "citizens" of the scientific ecosystem. By moving away from flat text retrieval and toward structured, multimodal knowledge orchestration, the framework provides the high-fidelity data that complex reasoning requires. [...] Image 1: Agents-K1 Framework Overview_Figure 1: The overall architecture of Agents-K1, integrating a multimodal knowledge graph layer, a reinforcement-learned extraction backbone, and an agent-native command-line interface._

Agents-K1 is a framework designed to address these deficiencies by creating "agent-native" knowledge graphs. Unlike traditional knowledge bases built for human readers, an agent-native system is structured specifically to support machine reasoning, providing fine-grained, auditable, and multimodal evidence. The system integrates a high-scale construction pipeline, a specialized extraction model, and a toolkit that allows agents to traverse these knowledge networks to conduct deep research.

## The Limitations of Current Knowledge Infrastructure [...] # Agents-K1: Towards Agent-native Knowledge Orchestration | alphaXiv

-based agents have shown significant potential in automating complex research tasks. However, a major bottleneck persists: while we have made strides in "agent orchestration"—the logic of how an agent plans and executes tasks—the underlying "knowledge orchestration" has lagged behind. Most research agents rely on basic Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) or citation graphs that capture only a fraction of scientific knowledge. These existing systems often ignore critical multimodal data like figures, tables, and equations, and they struggle to represent the nuanced relationships between different scientific claims.

results.title: Agents-K1: Towards Agent-native Knowledge Orchestration | alphaXiv
results.content:
John: A good connection. While both use reinforcement learning, their focus is different. Graph-R1 uses RL to optimize the agent's retrieval path through an existing graph. Agents-K1 uses RL to optimize the construction of the graph itself—the information extraction backbone. Agents-K1 is focused on building a much more detailed and multimodal knowledge source from the ground up. The 'GraphAnything CLI' it provides to agents is the interface to this richer world, fusing web search, multimodal graph retrieval, and cross-document traversal. The system's implication is a shift in how we think about the knowledge sources for AI. Instead of seeing scientific literature as a pile of PDFs for an agent to read at runtime, this framework treats it as a pre-compiled, structured, and multimodal [...] John: So, to wrap up, Agents-K1 presents a comprehensive pipeline for what it terms 'knowledge orchestration.' It moves beyond simple RAG by constructing a detailed, multimodal scientific knowledge graph at scale, using an efficient, reinforcement-learned model for extraction. The key takeaway is that to unlock the next level of scientific reasoning in AI agents, we must first build an agent-native knowledge infrastructure that reflects the complex, interconnected, and multimodal nature of science itself. Simply giving agents better search bars isn't enough.

John: Thanks for listening. If you have any further questions, ask our AI assistant or drop a comment. [...] John: An excellent point. They are tightly coupled, but the research focus has been asymmetric. Agent orchestration is about designing the agent's planning, tool-use, and execution logic. Knowledge orchestration is about designing the underlying knowledge base the agent queries. The core argument of Agents-K1 is that current knowledge systems, even advanced RAG pipelines, provide agents with fragmented, text-only information. They reduce papers to abstracts or simple citation links, ignoring the rich data in figures, tables, and the specific argumentative intent of a citation. This paper aims to fix that foundation.

results.title: InternScience/Agents-K1 · Hugging Face
results.content:
# Agents-K1

Knowledge extraction model in Agents-K1 is a 4B-parameter language model fine-tuned from
`Qwen/Qwen3-4B-Instruct-2507`
with GRPO (Group Relative Policy Optimization) on the information-extraction
corpus, targeting Named Entity Recognition (NER) and Relation Extraction (RE)
in English scientific and general-domain text.

`Qwen/Qwen3-4B-Instruct-2507`

The model produces structured JSON extractions with explicit step-by-step
reasoning, enabling its use as a building block in downstream knowledge-graph
construction, citation linking, and multi-hop QA pipelines.

## Highlights

`<think>…</think><answer>…</answer>`

## Intended use

Designed as an extraction backbone for:

Not intended for general-purpose chat — it has been specialized for structured
extraction.

## Usage [...] `<think>`
`<answer>`
`from transformers import AutoModelForCausalLM, AutoTokenizer
model_id = "InternScience/Agents-K1"
tok = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(model_id)
model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(model_id, torch_dtype="bfloat16", device_map="auto")
system = (
"You are an expert in information extraction. Given a task instruction "
"with schema definitions and input text, extract the required information.\n\n"
"You should think step by step about the extraction task, then provide "
"your answer in JSON format.\n\n"
"Format your response as:\n"
"<think>\nYour step-by-step reasoning...\n</think>\n"
"<answer>\nYour JSON extraction result here\n</answer>"
)
user = (

results.title: Explore | alphaXiv
results.content:
11 Jun 2026

Zongsheng Cao

Bihao Zhan

Jinxin Shi

Agents-K1 introduces a unified pipeline to construct agent-native scientific knowledge graphs from raw documents at scale, creating Scholar-KG from 2.46 million papers. The system enhanced multi-hop scientific reasoning for LLMs, with GPT-5.2's rationale accuracy increasing from 41.8% to 66.3% on geoscience research questions, and employs a 4B-parameter extraction model performing comparably to larger LLMs.
